event: Apgar score of 4 and 8 at 1 and 5 minutes, respectively
management protocol for the extremely preterm infant
late-onset sepsis secondary to Escherichia coli bacteremia
ulcer with extension to subcutaneous cell tissue
skin biopsy for microbiological and pathological studies
empiric antifungal treatment was initiated with liposomal amphotericin B (L-AmB)
critical clinical condition did not allow extensive surgical debridement of the affected tissue
fungal cultures obtained during skin biopsy, revealed the growth of a mold that was classified by phenotypic characteristics as Rhizopus spp.
histopathology report described the presence of broad aseptate hyphae with right angle branching, suggestive of Mucorales

Mass spectroscopy (matrix-assisted laser desorption/ ionization-time mass spectrometry of flight mass spectroscopy MALDI-TOF MS, BD™ Buker MALDI Biotyper™System) and polymerase chain reaction (PCR) plus sequencing from culture (panfungal PCR, sequencing region D1/ D2 -24S RNA long subunit-, internal transcribed spacer region) identified Rhizopus arrhizus (formerly R. oryzae) as the fungus responsible for the infection
Fungal blood cultures collected before starting L-AmB were negative
Necrotic ulcer extending up to the subcutaneous cellular tissue
Base of the ulcer with few broad and aseptate hyphae
Broad aseptate hyphae with right angle branching seen on skin ulcer
no extensive surgical debridement of the affected tissue
